source.span_hint: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees,

notes: Net tangible assets requirement; computed on restated and consolidated basis.
title: Net tangible assets ≥ ₹3 cr, not more than 50% in monetary assets
text: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), of which not more than fifty per cent. are held in monetary assets:

source.span_hint: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees,

notes: Three-year operating profit requirement; computed on restated and consolidated basis.
title: Operating profit ≥ ₹15 cr, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three years
text: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, during the preceding three years (of twelve months each), with operating profit in each of these preceding three years:

source.span_hint: it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees,

notes: Three-year net worth requirement; computed on restated and consolidated basis.
title: Net worth ≥ ₹1 cr in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each)
text: it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), calculated on a restated and consolidated basis:

source.span_hint: it has made an application to one or more

notes: Application for in-principle approval from stock exchange(s).
title: Application for in-principle approval
text: it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX;

source.span_hint: it has entered into an agreement with a depository

notes: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation of specified securities.
title: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation
text: it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued;

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form

notes: Specified securities held by promoters must be in dematerialised form before offer document filing.
title: Promoter's specified securities in dematerialised form
text: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document;

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up

notes: Existing partly paid-up equity shares must be fully paid-up or forfeited.
title: Fully paid-up equity shares or forfeited
text: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited;

source.span_hint: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered

notes: One-year holding period requirement for equity shares offered for sale.
title: Maximum holding period for equity shares offered for sale
text: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered for sale to the public, which have been held by the sellers for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document.

source.span_hint: Provided that in case the equity shares received

notes: Exceptions to the one-year holding period requirement for equity shares offered for sale arising from conversion or exchange of compulsorily convertible securities.
title: Exceptions to the holding period for equity shares offered for sale
text: Provided that in case the equity shares received on conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities including depository receipts are being offered for sale, the holding period of such convertible securities, including depository receipts, as well as that of resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of one year period referred in this sub-regulation.

source.span_hint: Provided further that such holding period

notes: Additional condition for the one-year holding period requirement for equity shares offered for sale: compliance at the time of filing of the draft offer document.
title: Additional conditions for the holding period of equity shares offered for sale
text: Provided further that such holding period of one year shall be required to be complied with at the time of filing of the draft offer document.

source.span_hint: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer

notes: No explicit conditions; eligibility inferred from text.
title: Issuer may issue convertible debt instruments without prior equity offer
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments even without making a prior public issue of its equity shares and listing thereof. Provided that it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public, if any, for a period of more than six months.

source.span_hint: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee

notes: Only condition (b) extracted; other conditions not represented as stable issuer/offer facts.
title: Issuer must appoint debenture trustee for convertible debt offer
text: In addition to other requirements laid down in these regulations, an issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall also comply with the following conditions: (b) it has appointed at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993.

source.span_hint: Provided that it is not in default of payment

notes: Default payment or repayment period for debt instruments issued to the public.
title: Default payment or repayment period
text: Provided that it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public, if any, for a period of more than six months.

source.span_hint: it has obtained credit rating

notes: Credit rating and appointment of debenture trustee requirements.
title: Credit rating and debenture trustee requirements
text: it has obtained credit rating from at least one credit rating agency; it has appointed at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993;

source.span_hint: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments

notes: Requires positive consent from holders for conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments.
title: Consent for conversion of convertible debt instruments
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion of any convertible debt instruments.

source.span_hint: Where the value of the convertible portion

notes: Gives holders of listed convertible debt instruments the option to not convert if conversion price is not determined.
title: Option to not convert convertible portion into equity shares
text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares.

source.span_hint: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed

notes: Warrant tenure limit; computed from allotment date.
title: Warrant tenure ≤ 18 months
text: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer;

source.span_hint: the price or formula for determination of exercise price

notes: Warrant exercise price formula and upfront payment requirement.
title: Warrant exercise price formula and upfront payment
text: the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront;

source.span_hint: in case the warrant holder does not exercise

notes: Warrant non-exercise forfeiture rule.
title: Warrant non-exercise forfeiture
text: in case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.

source.span_hint: For the purpose of this regulation:

notes: Computation method for promoters' contribution based on post-issue expanded capital, considering full conversion of convertible securities and exercise of all vested options.
title: Promoters' contribution computation
text: For the purpose of this regulation: (I) Promoters’ contribution shall be computed on the basis of the post-issue expanded capital: (a) assuming full proposed conversion of convertible securities into equity shares; (b) assuming exercise of all vested options, where any employee stock options or stock appreciation rights are outstanding at the time of initial public offer in terms of proviso (a) to sub-regulation (2) of regulation 5.

source.span_hint: For computation of ‘weighted average price’:

notes: Computation method for weighted average price, using the number of equity shares and predetermined conversion price.
title: Weighted average price computation
text: For computation of ‘weighted average price’: (a) ‘weight’ means the number of equity shares arising out of conversion of such specified securities into equity shares at various stages; (b) ‘price’ means the price of equity shares on conversion arrived at after taking into account the predetermined conversion price at various stages.

source.span_hint: Promoters’ contribution shall be computed

title: Promoters’ contribution based on post-issue expanded capital
text: Promoters’ contribution shall be computed on the basis of the post-issue expanded capital:

source.span_hint: assuming exercise of all vested options

title: Exercise of all vested options
text: (b) assuming exercise of all vested options, where any employee stock options or stock appreciation rights are outstanding at the time of initial public offer in terms of proviso (a) to sub-regulation (2) of regulation 5.

source.span_hint: pay to the issuer the difference between the price at which

notes: Entities paying difference for specified securities acquired; includes promoters, AIFs, FVCI, SCBs, PFIs, ICDAIs, and non-individual public shareholders.
title: Payment for specified securities acquired by certain entities
text: 41[if the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India] 42[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)] pay to the issuer the difference between the price at which the specified securities are offered in the initial public offer and the price at which the specified securities had been acquired.

source.span_hint: if such specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme

notes: Entities paying difference for specified securities acquired under sections 230-234 of the Companies Act.
title: Payment for specified securities acquired under sections 230-234 of the Companies Act
text: if such specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme under 43[***] sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, as approved by a High Court or a tribunal or the Central Government, as applicable, by the promoters in lieu of business and invested capital that had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to such approval;

source.span_hint: specified securities allotted to the promoters and alternative investment funds

notes: Entities paying difference for specified securities in government company, statutory authority or corporation IPOs.
title: Payment for specified securities in government company, statutory authority or corporation IPOs
text: specified securities allotted to the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India during the preceding one year at a price less than the issue price, in case of an issuer formed by conversion of one or more partnership firms or limited liability partnerships, where the partners of the erstwhile partnership firms or limited liability partnerships are the promoters of the issuer and there is no change in the management:

source.span_hint: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable

notes: Lock-in periods for specified securities held by promoters.
title: Lock-in of specified securities held by the promoters
text: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable (hereinafter referred to as “lock-in”) for the periods as stipulated hereunder:

source.span_hint: The specified securities held by the promoters shall not be transferable

notes: Lock-in period for promoter contribution; exception for capital expenditure.
title: Lock-in period for promoter contribution
text: The specified securities held by the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of eighteen months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer, except if the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be three years.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall be under lock-in until conversion

notes: Lock-in period for promoter excess holding; exception for capital expenditure.
title: Lock-in period for promoter excess holding
text: The promoters’ holding in excess of minimum promoters’ contribution shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer, except if the majority of the issue proceeds excluding the portion of offer for sale is proposed to be utilized for capital expenditure, then the lock-in period shall be one year.

source.span_hint: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters

notes: Lock-in period for equity shares held by persons other than promoters.
title: Lock-in of equity shares held by persons other than promoters
text: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: Provided that nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to

notes: Exceptions to lock-in for equity shares held by employees, employee stock option trusts, and stock appreciation rights schemes.
title: Exceptions to lock-in for equity shares held by persons other than promoters
text:
Provided that nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to: 
(a) equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, under an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme of the issuer prior to the initial public offer, if the issuer has made full disclosures with respect to such options or scheme in accordance with Part A of Schedule VI;
(b) equity shares held by an employee stock option trust or transferred to the employees by an employee stock option trust pursuant to exercise of options by the employees, whether currently employees or not, in accordance with the employee stock option plan or employee stock purchase scheme or a stock appreciation rights scheme.
Provided that the equity shares allotted to the employees shall be subject to the provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Share Based Employee Benefits and Sweat Equity) Regulations, 2021.

source.span_hint: Provided that such equity shares shall be locked in for a period of at least six months

notes: Lock-in period for equity shares held by venture capital funds or alternative investment funds.
title: Lock-in for equity shares held by venture capital funds or alternative investment funds
text: Provided that such equity shares shall be locked in for a period of at least six months from the date of purchase by the venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of Category I or Category II or foreign venture capital investor.

source.span_hint: Specified securities held by the promoters may be pledged

notes: Lock-in period for specified securities issued to promoters; applies after pledge invocation.
title: Lock-in for specified securities issued to promoters
text: The lock-in shall continue pursuant to the invocation of the pledge and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer the specified securities till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.

source.span_hint: Specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged

notes: Specified securities locked-in by promoters can be pledged as collateral for loans.
title: Pledge of locked-in specified securities
text: Specified securities68[, except SR equity shares,] held by the promoters and locked-in may be pledged as a collateral security for a loan granted by a scheduled commercial bank or a public financial institution or a systemically important non-banking finance company or a housing finance company, subject to the following:

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16

notes: Pledge of locked-in specified securities is allowed if the loan was granted to the issuer or its subsidiaries for financing the issue.
title: Conditions for pledge of locked-in specified securities (a)
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16, the loan has been granted to the issuer company or its subsidiary(ies) for the purpose of financing one or more of the objects of the issue and pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan;

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16

notes: Pledge of locked-in specified securities is allowed if the pledge was one of the terms of the loan sanction.
title: Conditions for pledge of locked-in specified securities (b)
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16 and the pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan.

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition

notes: Provisions for transfer of locked-in specified securities; subject to other SEBI regulations.
title: Transfer of locked-in specified securities
text: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition  of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, the specified securities69[, except SR equity shares,] held by the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 16, may be transferred to another promoter or any person of the promoter group or a new promoter and the specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation  17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred: Provided that the lock-in on such specified securities shall continue for the remaining period with the transferee and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer them till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall enter into an agreement with the lead manager(s)

notes: Agreement with lead manager(s) and other intermediaries.
title: Agreement with lead manager(s) and other intermediaries
text: The issuer shall enter into an agreement with the lead manager(s) in the format specified in Schedule II and enter into agreements with other intermediaries as required under the respective regulations applicable to the intermediary concerned: Provided that such agreements may include such other clauses as the issuer and the intermediaries may deem fit without diminishing or limiting in any way the liabilities and obligations of the lead manager(s).
